highlights.text: Estrogen levels typically peak during the mid-follicular phase, leading to the proliferative phase of the menstrual cycle.
The biopsy results show regular tubular endometrial glands with abundant mitotic figures, indicating a high rate of cell proliferation.
Estrogen is the primary hormone responsible for stimulating cell proliferation and growth in the endometrium during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle.
